cases.title: Scenario matrix row for GPS-required policy rejection when location unavailable
preconditions: Testing-plan §3 GPS validation block applies (FR-34, FR-35, BR-08/09/10, AC-09, AC-10).
gps-required-session fixture: Open class session with effective policy gpsRequired=true and valid QR token.
Authenticated enrolled student has no successful attendance record for the session.
steps: Resolve effective attendance policy for the open session and confirm gpsRequired=true.
Submit check-in command with valid qrToken but without usable GPS coordinates (permission denied or gps payload omitted).
Query persisted CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Self check-in is rejected with outcome GpsDisabled or GpsRequired per AC-09 coverage row; no Present/Late AttendanceRecord is created and a failed CheckInAttempt is logged with structured reason code.

cases.title: GPS policy module rejects check-in without attendance ledger write
preconditions: Real database transaction is enabled for policy resolution, check-in, and attendance ledger modules.
Open session exists with gpsRequired=true in effective policy; student is actively enrolled with no prior success record.
steps: Capture beforeState: zero Present/Late AttendanceRecord rows for (classSessionId, studentUserId).
Invoke check-in command with valid qrToken passing auth, session Open, token Valid, enrollment, and duplicate guards but omitting gps payload.
Query check_in_attempts and attendance_records within the same transaction boundary.
cases.expected: Policy evaluation at GPS guard step 6 persists one failed CheckInAttempt with outcome GpsRequired; AttendanceRecord row count remains zero and beforeState is unchanged.

cases.title: CheckInAttempt SM-04 terminates at GpsDisabled without attendance mutation
preconditions: Open gps-required session with valid qrToken exists in integration test database.
Authenticated enrolled student has no existing successful attendance for the session.
Client context simulates browser geolocation permission denied (no coordinates available to submit).
steps: Capture beforeState: session roster shows student in Pending with no success AttendanceRecord.
Submit check-in command that passes validation order steps 1–5 but cannot supply GPS due to permission denial.
Capture afterState for CheckInAttempt outcome and AttendanceRecord rows.
cases.expected: SM-04 evaluation reaches GpsDisabled terminal state at GPS policy guard; afterState shows persisted failed attempt with GpsDisabled and AttendanceRecord identical to beforeState (still Pending, no success write).

cases.title: Lecturer manual fallback remains available after GPS rejection
preconditions: Open gps-required session with enrolled student who has a failed GpsDisabled or GpsRequired CheckInAttempt and no success AttendanceRecord.
Assigned lecturer is within manual edit window per effective policy (BR-14).
Real database transaction is enabled for attendance correction and audit modules.
steps: Verify student AttendanceRecord remains Pending after GPS rejection attempt.
Lecturer invokes PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and required reason.
Query AttendanceRecord, AuditLog, and session roster counts.
cases.expected: Manual correction succeeds despite prior GPS rejection; student status becomes Manual Present with audit record containing actor, old/new status, reason, and timestamp per BR-14 and BR-22.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins returns 422 GpsRequired when gps payload omitted under GPS policy
preconditions: Open gps-required session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated student bearer token belongs to actively enrolled user with no prior success for the session.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and no gps field in body.
Inspect HTTP status, response envelope (data/meta/error), and error.code.
Verify no success attendance payload is returned.
cases.expected: API returns 422 with standard envelope where data is null and error.code is GpsRequired; response includes classSessionId in details where applicable and no Present/Late attendance row is created.

cases.title: E2E-05 alternate workflow branch rejects GPS-denied check-in with manual fallback path
preconditions: Lecturer has opened gps-required session (AC-01) and session is Open with rotating QR.
Student completes login gate (AC-06) and enrollment validation (AC-07) prerequisites.
Student device or test client simulates geolocation permission denied before submission.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/auth/login and receives Student role accessToken.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken but without usable GPS coordinates (permission denied scenario).
Lecturer queries GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for the student row and rejectedAttempts count.
Lecturer applies PATCH manual correction for the same student with Manual Present and documented reason.
cases.expected: Check-in fails with GpsDisabled (422); roster shows student Pending with rejectedAttempts incremented and failed attempt auditable per AC-18; lecturer manual fallback succeeds per BR-14 without bypassing audit requirements.

cases.title: Boundary error distinguishes GpsRequired missing payload from GpsDisabled permission denied
preconditions: Open gps-required session with valid qrToken exists.
Authenticated enrolled student has no successful attendance for the session.
Test harness can simulate both omitted gps field and explicit permission-denied client context.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with valid token but gps field entirely omitted; record status and error.code.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with valid token where client signals geolocation permission denied or unavailable; record status and error.code.
Verify AttendanceRecord row count and CheckInAttempt outcomes for both requests.
cases.expected: Omitted gps payload returns 422 with error.code GpsRequired; permission-denied scenario returns 422 with error.code GpsDisabled; neither path creates a success AttendanceRecord and both log structured failed attempts.

cases.title: Non-Student roles denied POST /v1/check-ins on GPS-required session
preconditions: Open gps-required session with valid QR token exists.
Lecturer and ITAdmin identities are available with valid bearer tokens per permission matrix.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins as Lecturer with valid qrToken and gps payload for the open session.
Call POST /v1/check-ins as ITAdmin with the same token context.
Call POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled Student (control) omitting gps under GPS-required policy.
Verify denial responses contain no partial attendance mutation from privileged roles.
cases.expected: Lecturer and ITAdmin receive 403 Forbidden before GPS policy evaluation; Student control reaches business validation and receives 422 GpsRequired or GpsDisabled; no AttendanceRecord is created by denied roles.

cases.title: UI journey surfaces GpsDisabled failure with recovery guidance and lecturer fallback hint
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser (PG-02 check-in page).
Target session has gpsRequired=true in effective policy and is Open with valid QR token.
Browser geolocation permission is denied or blocked for the test profile.
steps: Navigate to check-in URL with valid token query parameter for the gps-required session.
Observe GpsPermissionPrompt risk-reduction copy per FLOW-03 step 1.
Deny or block browser location permission when prompted.
Submit check-in from the student check-in form.
Observe CheckInResultScreen failure-gps-denied warning styling, recovery steps, and lecturer fallback guidance.
cases.expected: UI renders failure-gps-denied alert with localized message (e.g. 'Cần quyền vị trí'); recovery copy explains how to enable location and offers 'báo giảng viên' manual fallback path; no success status is shown and student remains Pending on roster.

cases.title: Lecturer roster shows GPS-rejected student as Pending with manual correction affordance
preconditions: Student has failed GpsDisabled check-in attempt on an Open gps-required session.
Lecturer is authenticated on desktop browser (PG-06 live roster view) for the assigned section.
Manual edit window is active per effective policy.
steps: Lecturer navigates to live session roster for the gps-required session.
Locate the student row showing Pending status with latestAttemptOutcome GpsDisabled in rejected summary.
Open ManualCorrectionDialog for the student.
Submit Manual Present correction with required reason text.
cases.expected: Roster displays student as Pending with GPS rejection visible in attempt summary; ManualCorrectionDialog opens and accepts correction; post-save row updates to Manual Present confirming lecturer fallback path remains operable per AC-09 and BR-14.
